##NAMES=Gotzenite
##RRUFFID=R070449
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_4_NaCa_2_Ti(Si_2_O_7_)_2_(OF)F_2_
##LOCALITY=Mont Saint-Hilaire, Rouville County, Quebec, Canada
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Michael Scott
##DESCRIPTION=Sprays of yellowish bladed crystals with green or colorless vesuvianite, white thick prismatic pectolite and brown phlogopite mica.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R070449
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 9.582 b: 5.718 c: 7.298 alpha: 89.87 beta: 101.05 gamma: 100.96 volume: 385 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
